questions.q: Kishkindha is best known as the kingdom of which people?
questions.explanation: Kishkindha is the mountain realm of the vanaras, the monkey-folk of the Ramayana.

questions.q: Who becomes the rightful king of Kishkindha with Rama's help?
questions.explanation: Rama kills Vali and installs Sugriva as king of Kishkindha.

questions.q: What was Kishkindha famous for in its terrain?
questions.explanation: Kishkindha was a mountain realm, associated with the hill Rishyamukha and rocky caves.

questions.q: Which famous vanara, minister to Sugriva, first meets Rama and Lakshmana near Kishkindha?
questions.explanation: Hanuman, Sugriva's minister, first approaches Rama and Lakshmana and brings them to Sugriva.

questions.q: Whom did Rama kill to give Kishkindha's throne to Sugriva?
questions.explanation: Rama slew Vali, Sugriva's brother and the reigning king, granting the throne to Sugriva.

questions.q: For what great purpose was the search launched from Kishkindha?
options: To find a cure for Lakshmana
questions.explanation: From Kishkindha, Sugriva sent vanara hosts in all directions to search for the abducted Sita.

questions.q: Vali and Sugriva, central to Kishkindha's story, were what to each other?
questions.explanation: Vali and Sugriva were brothers whose feud over the throne shaped Kishkindha's fate.

questions.q: In how many directions did Sugriva dispatch search parties from Kishkindha?
questions.explanation: Sugriva sent vanara parties to the four directions, north, south, east, and west, to seek Sita.

questions.q: The book of the Ramayana centered on these events is named the:
questions.explanation: The Kishkindha Kanda is the fourth book of Valmiki's Ramayana, set in the vanara kingdom.

questions.q: Whom did Rama treat as a friend and ally upon coming to Kishkindha?
questions.explanation: Rama formed an alliance of friendship with Sugriva, sealing it by fire.

questions.q: On which hill near Kishkindha did Sugriva take refuge while in exile from Vali?
questions.explanation: Sugriva hid on Rishyamukha hill, which Vali could not enter due to a curse from the sage Matanga.

questions.q: Why could Vali not pursue Sugriva onto Rishyamukha hill?
options: A curse of the sage Matanga
questions.explanation: The sage Matanga cursed Vali so he would die if he set foot on Rishyamukha, making it Sugriva's refuge.

questions.q: Who was the son of Vali, later a key vanara prince of Kishkindha?
questions.explanation: Angada, son of Vali, became a prominent vanara prince and leader in the search for Sita.

questions.q: What ornaments dropped by Sita helped the vanaras of Kishkindha identify her trail?
options: Jewels she cast down toward the vanaras
questions.explanation: While being carried off, Sita dropped her jewels among vanaras on a hill; these were later shown to Rama.

questions.q: Whom did Rama and Lakshmana recognize from the jewels brought before them at Kishkindha?
questions.explanation: Rama recognized Sita's ornaments, which Sugriva's vanaras had gathered after she dropped them.

questions.q: Who was the wise and eloquent queen, wife of Vali, in Kishkindha?
questions.explanation: Tara, Vali's queen, was renowned for her wisdom and counsel in Kishkindha.

questions.q: Whose wife had Vali taken, fueling Sugriva's grievance against him?
questions.explanation: Vali had seized Ruma, Sugriva's wife, deepening the brothers' bitter quarrel.

questions.q: What feat did Rama perform to convince Sugriva of his strength before the duel with Vali?
options: He pierced seven sala trees with one arrow
questions.explanation: Rama shot a single arrow through seven sala trees to assure Sugriva of his power against Vali.

questions.q: How did Rama signal which brother to support, since Vali and Sugriva looked alike in battle?
options: By a garland placed on Sugriva
questions.explanation: A garland (gajapushpi flowers) was placed on Sugriva so Rama could tell the near-identical brothers apart.

questions.q: After being crowned, Sugriva initially delayed the search for Sita because he was:
questions.explanation: Sugriva lingered in royal pleasures during the rains, prompting an angry reminder from Lakshmana.

questions.q: Which vanara of Kishkindha leads the crucial southern search party?
options: A party including Hanuman and Angada
questions.explanation: The southern party, including Hanuman, Angada, and Jambavan, found the trail leading to Lanka.

questions.q: Who reminded Sugriva, with great anger, of his duty to begin the search for Sita?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana came in wrath to Kishkindha and rebuked Sugriva for neglecting his promise to Rama.

questions.q: According to tradition, Kishkindha is often identified with a region near which modern site in Karnataka?
questions.explanation: Kishkindha is traditionally associated with the Hampi-Anegundi area along the Tungabhadra in Karnataka.

questions.q: Which aged and venerable bear-king of Kishkindha's host advised Hanuman to leap to Lanka?
questions.explanation: Jambavan, the wise king of bears, reminded Hanuman of his powers and urged him to leap across the ocean.

questions.q: Who guarded the honey-grove (Madhuvana) of Kishkindha that the returning vanaras plundered in joy?
questions.explanation: Dadhimukha, Sugriva's uncle, guarded Madhuvana, which the vanaras raided after finding Sita.

questions.q: From whose curse did the demon Kabandha's spirit guide Rama toward Sugriva at Kishkindha?
options: His own, after release by Rama
questions.explanation: Once freed by Rama from his cursed form, Kabandha advised Rama to seek Sugriva's alliance at Rishyamukha.

questions.q: The vanara craftsman of Kishkindha's army credited with building the bridge to Lanka was:
questions.explanation: Nala, son of Vishvakarma, was the vanara who engineered the great bridge (Setu) to Lanka.

questions.q: Where did the southern search party of Kishkindha encounter the ascetic Svayamprabha?
questions.explanation: The vanaras entered a magical cave (Riksha-bila) and were aided out by the ascetic Svayamprabha.

questions.q: Vali's strength was famously increased by a boon that did what during combat?
options: Drew half a foe's strength to himself when faced
questions.explanation: Vali's boon caused any opponent who faced him to lose half their strength to him, making him near-invincible head-on.

questions.q: The original quarrel between Vali and Sugriva began over a fight with which demon in a cave?
questions.explanation: Vali pursued the demon Mayavi into a cave; a misunderstanding there led Sugriva to seal it, sparking their feud.
